g.text: HERO LIFESTYLE INTERIOR SPACE REFERENCE (1720 x 440)
A Comprehensive Framework for Scalable Product Presentation
Developing operational standards across Photography, 3D Rendering, and Generative AI workflows.
MARIO MOJICA AFM — BRAND SYSTEM GUIDELINE

xhtml:div.xhtml:p: Over the past decade, Cylindo has worked with more than 300+ furniture retailers and manufacturers to produce visual content at scale across millions of product variations, channels, and customer touchpoints.
Consistency is the new limit. Most teams do not struggle to create visuals, but in creating visuals that remain consistent once production scales.

Photography, 3D rendering, AI image generation, and hybrid workflows are all becoming part of the same content ecosystem. Consistency does not break because teams lack talent. It breaks because there is no shared system guiding how visual decisions are made.

g.text: Brand Visual Systems — A Comprehensive Framework

foreignObject.xhtml:div: Consistent terminology for finishes, fabrics, materials, dimensions, and product features. Eliminates translation errors between design files and retail endpoints.
Clear rules for how products, variants, configurations, and collections are named across channels. Maps 1:1 to structural parameters of CAD models.
Guidance for adapting copy across PDPs, social channels, and marketplaces without diluting the core brand guidelines.

g.text: The Psychology of the Hesitation Gap

xhtml:div.xhtml:p: Understanding how visual realism builds trust. By standardizing photorealism, materials, and environment lighting, we close the customer's hesitation gap during remote online evaluations.

g.text: The quality of the image directly influences product perception. Poor presentation lowers value.
Online visuals must replicate the premium care, detail, and confidence of physical showrooms.
Visible quality (seams, wood grain alignment) helps customers validate higher pricing.
Compensates for the lack of physical touch.
Show enough detail, angles, and context for purchase.
Use realistic room scenes and physically plausible lighting.
Ensure the product shown online matches what customers expect.

foreignObject.xhtml:div: In furniture e-commerce, layout logic is about standardized visual presentation as much as interface design. It ensures customers can compare products accurately.

g.text: Products should be framed, scaled, and presented consistently so customers can compare silhouettes.
Product galleries should follow a consistent sequence: hero image, alternate angles, detail shots, and room-view tools.
Interactive features such as zoom, 360 views, configurators, or room-view tools should behave consistently.
Brand Visual Systems — A Comprehensive Framework

text.tspan: Unified UI components for image zoom, 360-degree

g.text: Limit scenes to 3 templates (e.g. Urban Loft, Studio). Floor oak.
Ceiling heights fixed 2.4 - 2.8m. Vertical lines remain straight.
Use explicit contact shadows on the floor/rug planes.
Limit minimal scenes to no more than 5 intentional props.

xhtml:div.xhtml:p: The challenge for modern furniture brands is no longer simply "producing content" but producing consistent, high-quality, on-brand content at a scale that meets the demands of the digital-first consumer.
A visual brand system is the infrastructure that reduces ambiguity, shortens production cycles, and protects perceived quality across all rendering, photography, and AI workflows.
Organizations that build structured visual systems will be best positioned to create consistent, trustworthy customer experiences across every channel, campaign, and technology.

g.text: Ready to turn visual consistency into a scalable system?
Book a demo to see how your product visuals can work harder.

MARIO MOJICA AFM — BRAND SYSTEM GUIDELINE

g.text: Exportación de archivos listos para maquinaria tipo Biesse y seccionadoras CNC.
Algoritmos para calcular coordenadas y hornear animaciones 3D procedimentales.
Cubicaje de empaques óptimos en cartón y distribución automática sobre la lámina.
REPORTE ANÁLITICO / FUNNEL DE ARMADO (1500 x 550)
Monitoreo automatizado de los pasos de ensamblado para detectar problemas físicos de fabricación o de claridad instructiva en el manual.
Disminución del 80% en reclamos telefónicos de postventa y optimización del branding del manual de armado al instante desde el CMS de Mario Mojica.
